title: Mass
short: The amount of matter in an object and its resistance to acceleration (inertia), measured in kilograms (kg).
full:
Mass measures how much matter an object contains and, just as importantly, how strongly it resists a change in motion — its inertia. The larger the mass, the more force is needed to produce a given acceleration (F = m·a).

Mass is not the same as weight. Mass is an intrinsic property and is the same everywhere; weight is the gravitational force on that mass, W = m·g, and changes with location (it is smaller on the Moon).

The SI unit of mass is the kilogram (kg).
